title: Bruins reportedly acquire defenseman Will Borgen in trade with Rangers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins acquired defenseman Will Borgen from the New York Rangers in exchange for future draft picks.
Borgen, 29, is expected to fill a top-four defensive role and has a $4.1 million cap hit for four years.
Borgen played 75 games for the Rangers last season, recording five goals and ten assists, averaging 18:03 ice time per game.

title: Celtics reportedly send Jaylen Brown to 76ers in stunning trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks.
Brown's departure follows a 10-season career with the Celtics, including an NBA Finals MVP win in 2024.
The trade includes two first-round and two second-round picks, concluding offseason trade rumors for Brown.

title: Sewage overflow issue in Merrimack River is resolved, officials say, although some beaches are still closed
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Heavy rainfall caused a wastewater main failure in Haverhill, releasing millions of gallons of sewage into the Merrimack River.
A temporary 24-inch bypass pipeline was installed, redirecting wastewater back to Haverhill’s treatment plant and stopping the river discharge.
Beaches downriver remain closed, despite the overflow ending, with health officials warning against river contact for 48 more hours.

title: Judge in Karen Read wrongful death suit probes leak of Proctor’s ‘sensitive’ information
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judge Mark Gildea investigates leak of former trooper Michael Proctor's private medical information in Karen Read wrongful death case.
Gildea orders temporary halt to Proctor's discovery, warns of potential permanent hold if leak issues persist.
Attorneys in the case deny involvement in leak; court hearing set for July 10 to address confidentiality breach.

title: These are the beaches closed in Mass. as the heat wave hits
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Several beaches in Massachusetts are closed due to high bacteria levels caused by recent sewage spills.
Sewer main breaks in Haverhill released untreated wastewater into the Merrimack River, affecting nearby beach water quality.
The state's interactive dashboard provides hourly updates on beach water quality from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. daily.

title: 17 books to read for America’s 250th anniversary
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston.com staff and local booksellers curated a list of 17 books for America's 250th anniversary.
The reading list includes histories of America's founding and critical examinations of the nation's past and present.
The selection features both new releases and modern classics relevant to the country's 250-year evolution.

title: Brockton to implement curfew in response to shootings after World Cup matches
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Brockton will implement a curfew on Friday night due to multiple shootings after Cape Verde's World Cup matches.
The curfew will run from 10 p.m. Friday to 5 a.m. Saturday, with alcohol sales ending at 9:30 p.m.
Brockton police seek National Guard support as part of efforts to prevent further street violence and disorder.

title: Bruins bring back Connor Clifton on 2-year contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed defenseman Connor Clifton to a two-year contract with a $2.25 million average annual cap hit.
Connor Clifton played the first five seasons of his NHL career with the Bruins before stints with the Sabres and Penguins.
Clifton's addition may end Andrew Peeke's tenure in Boston as the Bruins address their defensive roster needs.

title: 4 things to know about Mike Conley Jr. after reported one-year deal agreement with the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Mike Conley Jr. signs a one-year deal with the Boston Celtics, joining the team as a 38-year-old NBA veteran.
Conley is the son of Olympic gold medalist Mike Conley Sr. and previously played college basketball at Ohio State.
Conley has won the NBA Sportsmanship Award four times, the most of any player since its inception in 1996.

title: Alec Baldwin – at Tanglewood this weekend – talks ’30 Rock,’ Boston accents, and New England movies
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Alec Baldwin will narrate the Boston Symphony Orchestra's performance of 'Lincoln Portrait' at Tanglewood on July 5.
Baldwin, a part-time Vermont resident, is a fan of classical music and has attended Tanglewood events in the past.
The actor discussed his roles, love for classical music, and experiences with Boston and Rhode Island accents.
